# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(unmarked)
library(tidyverse)

# Step 1: Load the data
bird <- read.csv("PineRidge_BirdData.csv")

# Step 2: Specify the desired species
desired_species <- "bhco"  # Target species identifier

# Step 3: Create a new column indicating if the observation is the desired species
bird <- bird %>%
  mutate(is_desired_species = ifelse(species == desired_species, 1, 0))

# Step 4: Create a binary presence/absence column based on detection
bird <- bird %>%
  mutate(detected = ifelse(is_desired_species == 1 & !is.na(a.v), 1, 0))  # Adjust based on your detection criteria

# Step 5: Create the detection history matrix
detection_matrix <- bird %>%
  group_by(point, visit.num) %>%
  summarise(detected = max(detected, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = visit.num, values_from = detected, values_fill = 0)

# View the detection matrix
print(detection_matrix)
print(dim(detection_matrix))  # Check dimensions

bird <- bird %>%
  mutate(
    start = paste0(start, ":00"),  # Add ":00" to create "HH:MM:00"
    end = paste0(end, ":00")       # Add ":00" to create "HH:MM:00"
  )

# Step 2: Convert 'start' and 'end' times to hms (hours, minutes, seconds)
bird <- bird %>%
  mutate(
    start_time = hms(start),  # Convert "HH:MM:00" to hms format
    end_time = hms(end)       # Convert "HH:MM:00" to hms format
  )

bird <- bird %>%
  mutate(
    time_of_day = hour(start_time)  # Extract the hour as 'time_of_day'
  )

# Step 6: Create site covariates
site_covariates <- bird %>%
  group_by(point) %>%
  summarise(
    cover = first(cover),         # Cover type (e.g., forest, grassland)
    burn = first(burn),           # Burn type (e.g., burned, unburned)
    wind = mean(wind, na.rm = TRUE), # Average wind speed for each site
    date = first(date),           # Date of the visit
    time_of_day = mean(time_of_day, na.rm = TRUE), # Average time of day for each site (mean of start times)
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Check site covariates
print(site_covariates)
print(dim(site_covariates))  # Check dimensions

# Step 7: Create an unmarkedFrame for the occupancy model
detection_data <- as.matrix(detection_matrix[, -1])  # Exclude the point column

# Ensure detection_data is not empty
if (nrow(detection_data) == 0) {
  stop("The detection data is empty. Check your detection matrix.")
}

data_unmarked <- unmarkedFrameOccu(y = detection_data, siteCovs = site_covariates)
summary(data_unmarked)

# Step 8: Fit models
# 1. Basic Occupancy Model (Cover Type)
basic_model <- occu(~ wind + time_of_day ~ cover, data = data_unmarked)
summary(basic_model)

# 2. Full Model (Cover Type, Burn Type, Environmental Covariates)
full_model <- occu(~ wind + time_of_day ~ cover + burn, data = data_unmarked)
summary(full_model)

# 3. Interaction Model (Cover Type x Burn Type)
interaction_model <- occu(~ wind + time_of_day ~ cover * burn, data = data_unmarked)
summary(interaction_model)

# 4. Temporal Model (Including Date/Time)
temporal_model <- occu(~ wind + time_of_day + date ~ cover + burn, data = data_unmarked)
summary(temporal_model)

# 5. Null Model
null_model <- occu(~ wind + time_of_day + date ~ 1, data = data_unmarked)
summary(null_model)

# Step 9: Model Comparison using AIC
model_list <- list(
  basic_model = basic_model,
  full_model = full_model,
  interaction_model = interaction_model,
  temporal_model = temporal_model,
  null_model = null_model
)
aic_table <- data.frame(Model = character(), AIC = numeric(), k = integer())
for (model_name in names(model_list)) {
  model <- model_list[[model_name]]
  if (!is.null(model)) {
    log_lik <- logLik(model)
    k <- length(coef(model))  # Number of parameters
    aic <- -2 * log_lik + 2 * k
    aic_table <- rbind(aic_table, data.frame(Model = model_name, AIC = aic, k = k))
  }
}
aic_table <- aic_table %>% arrange(AIC)
print(aic_table)


# Step 10: Goodness-of-Fit (GOF) Test - Using Parametric Bootstrap for the Basic Model
# Define the deviance statistic function
dev_statistic <- function(model) {
  return(-2 * logLik(model))  # Deviance is -2*log-likelihood
}

# Perform the parametric bootstrap using the 'parboot' function
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
gof_test <- parboot(basic_model, nsim = 1000, statistic = dev_statistic)

# Print a summary of the GOF test results
summary(gof_test)
# Plot the results of the GOF test to visualize the bootstrap deviances
plot(gof_test)


# Step 11: Residuals Analysis for Basic Model
# Get residuals
residuals_basic <- residuals(basic_model)

# Plot residuals
plot(residuals_basic, main = "Residuals of Basic Model", ylab = "Residuals", xlab = "Index")
abline(h = 0, col = "red")


# Generate predictions for occupancy (state) probabilities using site_covariates
occupancy_pred <- predict(basic_model, type = "state", newdata = site_covariates)

# Check the first few predictions to ensure they're generated
head(occupancy_pred)


# Create a data frame for plotting with the predicted occupancy probabilities
occupancy_pred_df <- data.frame(
  cover = site_covariates$cover,  # Covariate for x-axis (Cover Type)
  occupancy_prob = occupancy_pred  # Predicted occupancy probabilities
)

head(occupancy_pred_df)


# Generate predictions with standard error for occupancy (state) and detection
occupancy_pred_se <- predict(basic_model, type = "state", newdata = site_covariates, se.fit = TRUE)
summary(occupancy_pred_se)
# Check the structure of the returned object
str(occupancy_pred_se)
# Check if predictions are generated
head(occupancy_pred_se$Predicted)  # This should give you predicted occupancy probabilities
head(occupancy_pred_se$SE)  # This should give you standard errors

# Create a data frame for plotting occupancy predictions
occupancy_pred_df <- data.frame(
  cover = site_covariates$cover,  # Cover types
  occupancy_prob = occupancy_pred_se$Predicted,  # Predicted occupancy probabilities
  se = occupancy_pred_se$SE  # Standard error of predictions
)

# Check the structure of the data frame
head(occupancy_pred_df)

ggplot(occupancy_pred_df, aes(x = cover, y = occupancy_prob)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = occupancy_prob - se, ymax = occupancy_prob + se),  # Add error bars
    width = 0.2, 
    color = "red"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Occupancy Probability by Cover Type",
    x = "Cover Type",
    y = "Occupancy Probability"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
head(occupancy_pred_df)
summary(occupancy_pred_df$cover)




# Generate predictions with standard error for detection (type = "det")
detection_pred_se <- predict(basic_model, type = "det", newdata = site_covariates, se.fit = TRUE)

# Check the structure of the returned object
str(detection_pred_se)

# Check if predictions are generated
head(detection_pred_se$Predicted)  # This should give you predicted detection probabilities
head(detection_pred_se$SE)  # This should give you standard errors

# Create a data frame for plotting detection predictions
detection_pred_df <- data.frame(
  cover = site_covariates$cover,  # Cover types
  detection_prob = detection_pred_se$Predicted,  # Predicted detection probabilities
  se = detection_pred_se$SE  # Standard error of predictions
)


# Check the structure of the data frame
head(detection_pred_df)

# Display summary of the detection prediction data
summary(detection_pred_df)








new_data <- data.frame(
  wind = site_covariates$wind,        # Wind data (replace with your covariate data)
  time_of_day = site_covariates$time_of_day  # Time of day (replace with your covariate data)
)

# Predict detection probabilities
detection_probabilities <- predict(basic_model, type = "det", newdata = new_data)

# Print the prediction results
print(detection_probabilities)
# Predict detection probabilities using the fitted model
detection_pred <- predict(basic_model, type = "det", newdata = new_data)


# Calculate the p-value by comparing t0 to t.star
p_value <- mean(gof_test@t.star >= gof_test@t0)
p_value

# Create a table with the observed test statistic and p-value
gof_summary <- data.frame(
  "Observed Test Statistic" = gof_test@t0,
  "P-Value" = p_value
)

# Print the summary table
print(gof_summary)
library(AICcmodavg)
# Perform the goodness-of-fit test on the fitted occupancy model
gof_test <- mb.gof.test(basic_model)
summary(gof_test)
gof_test$chi.square
gof_test$p.value
# Extract chi-square statistic and p-value
gof_result <- data.frame(
  Model = "Basic Occupancy Model",  # You can update this with your model name
  Chi_Square = gof_test$chi.square,
  P_Value = gof_test$p.value
)

# Print the table
print(gof_result)
